items.title: Incoherent AGI Hype Spurs An Industrywide Pivot To Hybrid AI - Forbes
items.titleZh: Incoherent AGI Hype Spurs An Industrywide Pivot To Hybrid AI - Forbes
items.snippet: While genAI possesses remarkable capabilities and offers substantial commercial value, it currently faces a critical reliability challenge. In automated enterprise workflows, such as customer service interactions or healthcare claims proces
items.snippetZh: While genAI possesses remarkable capabilities and offers substantial commercial value, it currently faces a critical reliability challenge. In automated enterprise workflows, such as customer service interactions or healthcare claims proces
items.content: While genAI possesses remarkable capabilities and offers substantial commercial value, it currently faces a critical reliability challenge. In automated enterprise workflows, such as customer service interactions or healthcare claims processing, a minor error rate – even as low as 5% or less – can render a fully autonomous system non-viable due to the operational risks of factual inaccuracies, ethical missteps or mishandled transactions. Hybrid AI: A Practical Antidote To The Hype So, how do we sober up and pursue feasible deployments that realize the potential value of these technologies? The answer is hybrid AI. The Prompt: Get the week’s biggest AI news on the buzziest companies and boldest breakthroughs, in your inbox. Email Address Sign Up [...] You’re Subscribed! Explore More Newsletters Hybrid AI offers a practical alternative to pursuing the ever-elusive ideal AGI. GenAI hallucinates and exhibits other unacceptable behaviors that preclude its deployment – especially for its mor

items.title: OpenAI and Google employees rush to Anthropic’s defense in DOD lawsuit - TechCrunch
items.titleZh: OpenAI and Google employees rush to Anthropic’s defense in DOD lawsuit - TechCrunch
items.snippet: Late last week, the Pentagon labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk — usually reserved for foreign adversaries — after the AI firm refused to allow the Department of Defense to use its technology for mass surveillance of Americans or autonom
items.snippetZh: Late last week, the Pentagon labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk — usually reserved for foreign adversaries — after the AI firm refused to allow the Department of Defense to use its technology for mass surveillance of Americans or autonom
items.content: Late last week, the Pentagon labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk — usually reserved for foreign adversaries — after the AI firm refused to allow the Department of Defense to use its technology for mass surveillance of Americans or autonomously firing weapons. The DOD had argued that it should be able to use AI for any “lawful” purpose and not be constrained by a private contractor. The amicus brief in support of Anthropic showed up on the docket a few hours after the Claude maker filed two lawsuits against the DOD and other federal agencies. Wired was first to report the news. [...] ### Save up to $300 or 30% to TechCrunch Founder Summit #### 1,000+ founders and investors come together at TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 for a full day focused on growth, execution, and real-world scaling. Learn from founders and investors who have shaped the industry. Connect with peers navigating similar growth stages. Walk away with tactics you can apply immediately Offer ends March 13. San Francisco
items.contentZh: Late last week, the Pentagon labeled Anthropic a supply chain risk — usually reserved for foreign adversaries — after the AI firm refused to allow the Department of Defense to use its technology for mass surveillance of Americans or autonomously firing weapons. The DOD had argued that it should be able to use AI for any “lawful” purpose and not be constrained by a private contractor. The amicus brief in support of Anthropic showed up on the docket a few hours after the Claude maker filed two lawsuits against the DOD and other federal agencies. Wired was first to report the news. [...] ### Save up to $300 or 30% to TechCrunch Founder Summit #### 1,000+ founders and investors come together at TechCrunch Founder Summit 2026 for a full day focused on growth, execution, and real-world scaling. Learn from founders and investors who have shaped the industry. Connect with peers navigating similar growth stages. Walk away with tactics you can apply immediately Offer ends March 13. items.title: OpenAI robotics head resigns over Pentagon deal - The Robot Report
items.titleZh: OpenAI robotics head resigns over Pentagon deal - The Robot Report
items.snippet: OpenAI announced its deal with the DoD at the end of February. The news came just days after Anthropic ended its own discussions with the DoD. As part of its agreement with the DoD, OpenAI is allowing its technology to be used in classified
items.snippetZh: OpenAI announced its deal with the DoD at the end of February. The news came just days after Anthropic ended its own discussions with the DoD. As part of its agreement with the DoD, OpenAI is allowing its technology to be used in classified
items.content: OpenAI announced its deal with the DoD at the end of February. The news came just days after Anthropic ended its own discussions with the DoD. As part of its agreement with the DoD, OpenAI is allowing its technology to be used in classified environments. Sam Altman, the CEO of OpenAI, claimed that the company’s contract includes protections preventing the DoD from using the company’s technology for surveillance or autonomous weapons. --- #### About The Author ##### Brianna Wessling [...] Kalinowski joined OpenAI in November 2024 to lead the company’s renewed hardware and robotics group. While at the company, Kalinowski helped to build the company’s robotics group as it scaled. Before working at OpenAI, Kalinowski led the Meta team building augmented reality glasses. Before joining OpenAI, Kalinowski worked at Meta, Oculus VR, Apple, and more. | Source: Meta ## OpenAI’s defense agreement Kalinowski’s resignation comes as AI’s role in military operations becomes an increasingly heated di
items.contentZh: OpenAI announced its deal with the DoD at the end of February. The news came just days after Anthropic ended its own discussions with the DoD. As part of its agreement with the DoD, OpenAI is allowing its technology to be used in classified environments. Sam Altman, the CEO of OpenAI, claimed that the company’s contract includes protections preventing the DoD from using the company’s technology for surveillance or autonomous weapons. --- #### About The Author ##### Brianna Wessling [...] Kalinowski joined OpenAI in November 2024 to lead the company’s renewed hardware and robotics group. While at the company, Kalinowski helped to build the company’s robotics group as it scaled. Before working at OpenAI, Kalinowski led the Meta team building augmented reality glasses. Before joining OpenAI, Kalinowski worked at Meta, Oculus VR, Apple, and more. | Source: Meta ## OpenAI’s defense agreement Kalinowski’s resignation comes as AI’s role in military operations becomes an increasingly heated di
